title: Crisis Support: Mental Health Resources | Sarah Olney MP - Member of Parliament for Richmond Park
meta_description: Resources and support services in Richmond upon Thames and Kingston for anyone struggling with their mental health...
headings: h3: Crisis Support: Mental health resources and helplines
text_excerpt: --> Crisis Support: Mental Health Resources | Sarah Olney MP - Member of Parliament for Richmond Park top of page BOOK A SARAH OLNEY EVENT SUBSCRIBE TO MY NEWSLETTER PHONE: 020 8876 2986 EMAIL: sarah.olney.mp@parliament.uk Home News Campaigns Petition: Richmond Police Station Hammersmith Bridge Banning Night Flights Standing up to Thames Water Constituent Services Constituent Assistance Voting: Registering and ID Links and Resources Crisis Support: Mental health resources Crisis Support: Cost of Living Crisis Support: Domestic Violence Sarah's Priorities Contact Subscribe More... Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Crisis Support: Mental health resources and helplines ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Emergencies: If you feel you cannot keep yourself safe, if you have harmed yourself, or you feel you are at immediate risk of harming yourself or others, ring 999 for an ambulance or go to A&E (Kingston Hospital NHS Trust, Galsworthy Road, Kingston Upon Thames, Surrey, KT2 7QB). Other Services ​ Local Recovery Cafes Recovery cafes support people in reducing immediate distress, anxiety, or crisis and to safely plan how to manage future difficulties. ​ 966 Garratt Lane, Tooting, SW17 0ND. Tel: 07794 394 920. Email: recoverycafe@hestia.org 296 Kingston Road, SW20 8LX. Tel: 07908 436 617. Email: info@sunshinerecoverycafe.org The Maytree The Maytree is a London based charity that supports people through suicidal period, offering a one-off 4 night/5 day stay. 020 7263 7070 10am-6pm daily. Link to website here maytree@maytree.org.uk Richmond Mind Richmond Mind offer face-to-face crisis support in the evenings for Kingston and Richmond residents at Journey Recovery Hubs. To self-refer please complete the self-referral form here or get in touch on 020 3137 9755 or recoveryhub@rbmind.org Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday (6-10pm), Saturday (2-10pm) at the Alfriston Centre, 3 Berrylands Road, Surbiton, KT5 8RB. Tuesday, Thursday, Friday (6-10pm), Sunday (2-8pm) at 32 Hampton Road, Twickenham, TW2 5QB. Papyrus Papyrus for children and young people aged under 35 years old, a confidential support and advice service for those concerned about someone who is feeling suicidal and for those thinking about suicide. Link to website here . Call: 0800 068 4141 Text: 07860 039 967 Email: pat@papyrus-uk.org Shout A 24/7 te xt support service. Text Shout to 85258 Link to website here Saneline Saneline offers specialist emotional support and information to anyone affected by mental health difficulties, including family, friends and carers. Tel: 0845 767 8000 (every evening, 6pm – 11pm) Email support: www.sane.org.uk Support forum: www.sane.org.uk ​ NHS - Every Mind Matters Take the short quiz in the below link to help you create an action plan to help with your mental health Link to website here ​ Togetherall Online support for young people in South West London Link to website here ​ Talking therapies Find free talking therapies in your local area via the link below NHS talking therapies ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Contact your GP or Care Coordinator if you have one. They are your first port of call if you are feeling worse, both physically and mentally, or if you feel you require medication or need to change your medication. Ask for an urgent/emergency appointment if necessary. South West London and St George’s Mental Health Trust Crisis Support 24/7 Mental Health Crisis Line - 0800 028 8000. For a child or young person contact the SLP CAMHS Crisis Line on 0203 228 5980, Monday to Friday 5pm – 11pm, and Saturday, Sunday, and BHs 9am – 11pm. Link to their website here. Get in Touch with Your MP VIRTUAL COFFEE MORNING CONTACT SARAH SUBSCRIBE As the Liberal Democrat MP for Richmond Park, I am a committed advocate for liberal values of inclusivity, opportunity and equality. I am proud to be the voice of Richmond Park in Westminster, working hard on behalf of all of my constituents. Details about our data protection and privacy policies are
